back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'The Doe of the Dawn.' A psalm of David.
My God, my God, why have You forsaken me? Why are You so far off, not saving me, far from my words of groaning?
O my God, I cry out by day, but You do not answer; and by night I have no rest.
Yet You are holy, enthroned on the praises of Israel.
Our fathers trusted in You; they trusted, and You delivered them.
They cried out to You and were rescued; they trusted in You and were not put to shame.
But I am like a worm and not a man, scorned by men and despised by the people.
All who see me mock me; they sneer and shake their heads at me, saying,
'He commits himself to the LORD; let Him deliver him; let Him rescue him, since He delights in him.'
Yet You are the One who brought me forth from the womb; You made me trust even at my mother's breast.
From birth I was cast upon You; from my mother's womb You have been my God.
Do not be far from me, for trouble is near and there is no one to help.
Many bulls surround me; strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.
They open their mouths against me, like a lion mauling its prey and roaring.
I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint; my heart melts like wax within my breast.
My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and my tongue clings to the roof of my mouth; and You lay me in the dust of death.
For dogs surround me; a band of evil men encircles me; they have pierced my hands and my feet.
I count all my bones; the people stare and gloat over me.
They divide my garments among them, and cast lots for my clothing.
But You, O LORD, do not be far off; O my Strength, come quickly to help me.
Deliver my life from the sword, my precious life from the paw of the dog.
Save me from the mouth of the lion; You have answered me from the horns of the wild oxen.
I will proclaim Your name to my brothers; I will praise You in the midst of the assembly.
You who fear the LORD, praise Him! All offspring of Jacob, glorify Him! All offspring of Israel, stand in awe of Him!
For He has not despised or abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; and when he cried to Him, He heard; He has not hidden His face from him.
My praise in the great assembly comes from You; I will fulfill my vows before those who fear Him.
The humble will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the LORD will praise Him. May your hearts live forever.
All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the LORD, and all the families of the nations will bow in worship before You.
For the kingship belongs to the LORD, and He rules over the nations.
All the rich of the earth will eat and worship; all who go down to the dust will kneel before Him—those who cannot keep their own lives alive.
Offspring will serve Him; the Lord will be told of to the coming generation.
They will come and proclaim His righteousness to a people yet to be born, that He has accomplished it.
